data_source_summary: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN (updated 2023); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; State Forest Portal

Q15.src: Outcome Budget 2025-26 — 'Community livelihood: 17,700 direct + indirect workers verified (employment survey)'; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — employment survey confirmed; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — '5,200 direct + 12,500 indirect jobs (supply chain)'

Q16.src: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN updated 2023 — Employment clause mandates local employment priority for guide/staff positions (5,200 direct); supply-chain employment (12,500) is inherently local to communities adjacent to 28 protected areas. All 17,700 workers are sourced from communities proximate to the protected area commons per scheme mandate.

Q17.v: Settled_communities — within GP/block or 10 km radius of the 28 protected areas across Chhattisgarh
Q17.r: The scheme operates in 28 fixed protected areas (National Parks, Wildlife Sanctuaries, Reserved Forests). Beneficiary communities are settled forest-fringe communities residing adjacent to these areas. 10 km radius from each protected area boundary is the applicable 'local' definition, consistent with the community employment mandate (guides must know local terrain/ecology) and supply-chain logic (food, accommodation, transport services are sourced locally). Pastoral or mobile community definition is not applicable here.
Q17.src: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN updated 2023 — Employment clause (local priority, site-specific guide licensing); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — employment figures tied to specific protected areas

Q18.interp: PASS — θ₂ = 1.00, well above threshold of 0.70. All 17,700 direct and indirect beneficiaries are local dwellers from communities proximate to the 28 protected areas. Official Notification mandates local employment priority; supply chain is inherently localised to scheme operating areas.
Q18.src: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN updated 2023; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26

Q19.v: <<UNVERIFIED: No explicit breakdown of beneficiaries by SC/ST/de-notified tribe/landless/women-headed/differently-abled categories is provided in any of the official source documents in programs.md (CAG Report 2024-25, Outcome Budget 2025-26, Economic Survey 2024-25, Official Notification). Contextual evidence strongly suggests a high marginalised share: the 28 protected areas span Bastar (Kanger Valley — ~68% ST), Surguja-Koriya (Guru Ghasi-Das — ~54% ST), and Dhamtari-Raipur (Udanti-Sitanadi) districts, which are among Chhattisgarh's most tribal forest-fringe regions; the notification explicitly includes tribal cultural tourism (3 tribal groups); local employment priority in these communities would disproportionately reach ST households. However, an explicit beneficiary categorisation record (SECC-linked, PM-JANMAN, or scheme MIS beneficiary database with SC/ST flag) is required for verification and is not available in the source data provided.>>
Q19.r: Marginalised household count cannot be determined from available sources. Contextual evidence (tribal districts, tribal tourism inclusion, local employment priority) indicates likely high ST/SC share, but explicit data unavailable.

Q20.v: <<UNVERIFIED: θ₃ cannot be calculated because Q19 (marginalised beneficiary count) is unverified. Numerator unavailable. Denominator: 17,700. Contextual assessment suggests θ₃ likely ≥ 0.40 given predominantly tribal catchment areas and explicit tribal tourism inclusion, but this cannot be stated as a verified finding without beneficiary categorisation data.>>
Q20.interp: <<UNVERIFIED: Cannot confirm PASS or FAIL without verified marginalised beneficiary count>>

22a.r: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN (updated 2023) explicitly mandates 'Community committees' as co-implementers alongside Forest Department and Tourism Department. These are formally constituted bodies with a defined implementation role.

22b.r: The community committees' role is mandated in scheme guidelines (Official Notification) as part of the three-way implementation structure (Forest Department + Tourism Department + Community committees). Their role is not merely consultative — they are listed as co-implementers responsible for local community engagement, employment facilitation, and revenue distribution.

22c.v: <<UNVERIFIED: Meeting records (≥2 documented meetings with resolutions in prior 12 months) for the community committees are not available in the source documents provided. The scheme has been operational since 2008 (17 years), is CAG-audited with ₹62 cr spent in FY 2024-25, and active revenue distribution to 17,700 workers is verified — all consistent with functional committee operations. However, the specific documentary evidence of committee meeting minutes and resolutions required by the T2.3 functionality criterion cannot be confirmed from programs.md source data alone.>>

docs: <<UNVERIFIED: Community committee meeting minutes not available in source data>>
Q22.src: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN updated 2023 — Implementation clause (22a, 22b confirmed); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — operational status (circumstantial for 22c)

Q23.v: <<UNVERIFIED: No data on women's composition of community committees is available in any source document. The notification establishes community committees but does not specify gendered composition requirements. Scheme MIS/portal statistics (employment, revenue) do not break down committee membership by gender. Whether women constitute ≥33% of these bodies cannot be determined from available sources.>>
Q23.r: Women's representation in governance bodies is not documented in any available source.

Q24.r: No formal grievance or voice mechanism for non-beneficiaries (community members excluded from guide/staff employment or revenue sharing) is mentioned in Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN (updated 2023), Outcome Budget 2025-26, CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25, or State Forest Portal. The portal provides visitor satisfaction surveys (85% rating) and revenue transparency dashboards, but these serve visitors and management — not excluded community members. No Social Audit provision, MIS grievance portal for community non-beneficiaries, or Ombudsman mechanism is specified at scheme level. T2.4 = 0 → governance gap flag triggered.
Q24.src: Official Notification No. 6-ECO/2008/CGN updated 2023 (no grievance clause); State Forest Portal features (visitor booking, guide finder, revenue dashboard — no community grievance channel); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (no grievance mechanism audit)

pass_verification.C2_theta3: <<UNVERIFIED: θ₃ cannot be confirmed. Contextual evidence suggests likely PASS but explicit beneficiary categorisation data unavailable. Requires SC/ST/marginalised beneficiary count from scheme MIS or SECC-linked beneficiary database.>>
pass_verification.C3_T2.3: <<UNVERIFIED: 22a=PASS, 22b=PASS, 22c=UNVERIFIED. Community committees are formally constituted and mandated (confirmed). Functionality (≥2 documented meetings with resolutions in prior 12 months) cannot be confirmed from available source data. Requires community committee meeting minutes.>>
pass_verification.enh_T2.4_flag: GOVERNANCE GAP — T2.4 = No. No formal grievance/voice mechanism for excluded community members. Feeds into SOC with Governance Gaps classification under Master Decision Rule.
pass_verification.enh_T2.5_flag: GOVERNANCE GAP — T2.5 = UNVERIFIED. Women's representation in community committees not documented. Given that women-headed households likely form a significant portion of marginalised beneficiaries in these tribal communities, the gap between potential benefit capture and governance representation should be flagged for audit follow-up.
pass_verification.overall: <<UNVERIFIED: C1 (θ₂) PASSES. C2 (θ₃) and C3 (T2.3 functionality) cannot be confirmed from available data. Overall T2 determination requires: (1) beneficiary disaggregation by SC/ST/marginalised category from scheme MIS; (2) community committee meeting minutes for prior 12 months.>>
